ground_truth:
ULTRASOUND ABDOMEN (COMPLETE)

Date: 17/01/2024
Patient: Anna Richardson  DOB: 25/08/1975
MRN: 667834
Indication: RUQ pain, evaluate gallbladder.

Technique: Real-time ultrasound of the abdomen was performed.

Liver:
Normal size and echotexture. No focal lesion.
Hepatic and portal veins are patent.
Common bile duct measures 4 mm (normal).

Gallbladder:
Well-distended, thin walls. No gallstones or sludge.
No pericholecystic fluid. Positive Murphy sign.
No cholecystitis.

Pancreas:
Head, body, and tail visualized. Normal.
Pancreatic duct is not dilated.

Spleen:
Normal size at 10.2 cm. Homogeneous echotexture.

Kidneys:
Right kidney: 11.2 cm, normal cortex and echogenicity. No hydronephrosis.
Left kidney: 11.5 cm, normal. No hydronephrosis.
No renal calculi identified.

Aorta:
Normal caliber, no aneurysm.

Impression:
1. Unremarkable complete abdominal ultrasound.
2. No evidence of cholelithiasis or cholecystitis.
3. Normal liver, pancreas, spleen, kidneys, and aorta.

Sonographer: Rebecca Taylor
Supervising: Dr. Mark Stevens, MD
